headline: Survey at 12% completion with strong regional concentration and clear expansion mandate.
collection_status: 161 of 1,350 responses collected (11.9% progress). LINDI dominates with 40% of responses; 7 of 10 target regions active. Current pace suggests 18-month trajectory to target without acceleration.
key_findings: Expansion is the dominant priority: 58 respondents (36%) explicitly prioritize expanding KiuFunza; new programs and policy reform together equal 71 requests, signaling readiness for growth.

Field-level engagement strong but incomplete: Teachers represent 75 of 161 (47%); Head Teachers 36 (22%); but District Education Officers (5), Regional Coordinators (5), and Regional Education Officers (2) are severely underrepresented, risking blind spots in scalability planning.

Scale readiness significantly lags satisfaction: Partner satisfaction averages 4.5/5, but scale readiness averages only 2.7/5—a 1.8-point gap indicating confidence in current work but anxiety about expansion capacity.

Feedback themes unmistakable: Teachers demand regular incentives, digital monitoring systems, and stakeholder communication; policymakers need sustained government engagement and integration into national education policy.
flags: Geographic imbalance critical: LINDI (64) represents 40% of sample; SINGIDA (1), MTWARA (8), SONGWE (8) require urgent catch-up collection to ensure representative policy insights.

District and regional leadership severely undersampled: Only 12 responses from District/Regional Education Officers (DLs, REOs, ARCs combined); risk of scaling blockers undetected at administrative level.

Scale readiness gap is a risk signal: 2.7/5 suggests on-ground teams uncertain about systems, staffing, or resources needed for growth—expansion plans may face implementation friction without addressing this.
recommendation: Immediately intensify collection in underrepresented regions (MTWARA, SONGWE, SINGIDA, TANGA) and prioritize District/Regional leader interviews (target: 30+ additional responses from education officials) to validate expansion feasibility and policy pathway before scaling decisions.
